laws.title: Public Law 17-135 | Judicial Bias Protection Act (JBPA)
laws.final_statutory_text: Section 5 repeals and replaces 28 U.S.C. 144, narrowing the affidavit-of-bias procedure and adding timing and reassignment limits; sections 4, 6, and 7 are free-standing policy provisions.
laws.provision_level_disposition: Direct amendment to 28 U.S.C. 144; no separate Code placement for sections 4, 6, or 7.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is intact and operative, and nothing in the reviewed corpus repeals Public Law 17-135 itself.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Section 5 is a direct replace-section-text operation against 28 U.S.C. 144; no transfer or redesignation chain is involved.
laws.status_basis: The decision memorandum says section 5 repeals and replaces 28 U.S.C. 144, and the current branch has the USAR amendment note at that section. The substantive section text still needs the replacement applied.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML has the Pub. L. 17-135 history note, but the body of 28 U.S.C. 144 is still the pre-amendment text from origin/main.

source_evidence: codification/decisions/PL-017-135.md says section 5 repeals and replaces 28 U.S.C. 144.
usc/usc28.xml in the current branch contains the USAR note for Pub. L. 17-135, section 5.
git diff origin/main shows that note was added on this branch.
recommended_actions: Apply the section 5 replacement text to 28 U.S.C. 144.
Preserve the heading, source credit, and historical notes.
Remove the Trello link once the text replacement is integrated.

laws.title: Public Law 17-136 | The Parks Act (TPA)
laws.final_statutory_text: Grants the Secret Service primary authority over President's Park and White House grounds, regulates limited closures and notice, and assigns secondary enforcement authority to other law-enforcement agencies.
laws.provision_level_disposition: Standalone generally permanent law; best preserved as a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 4577.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is intact and permanent, and no later enactment in the reviewed set repeals it.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal, transfer, or redesignation chain was identified.
laws.status_basis: The law text is readable and permanent, but it does not give a uniquely executable Code amendment. The best placement is a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 4577.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML has the preexisting section 4577 text, but no PL-17-136 statutory note has been added.

source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/reports/laws/PL-017-136.md classifies the act as STATUTORY_NOTE at 2 U.S.C. 4577.
codification/mass_migration/latest/sources/text/PL-017-136.txt contains the Secret Service / President's Park closure provisions.
usc/usc02.xml currently has 2 U.S.C. 4577 but no PL-17-136 note.
recommended_actions: Add a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 4577.
Do not create a new Code section.
Do not alter XML text beyond the note insertion.

laws.title: Public Law 17-137 | Federal Background Check Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Requires FBI-approved background checks for federal agency employment, sets timing and waiver rules, and voids employment obtained without a qualifying check; the enactment itself is marked repealed in the source set.
laws.provision_level_disposition: No Code operation survives because the law is treated as repealed/nonoperative.
laws.chronology: Repealed enactment. The source report classifies the law as repealed, and no current Code placement is authorized.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No transfer or redesignation issue; the law is excluded from current Code treatment.
laws.status_basis: The source report classifies the Trello record as repealed and the law text itself is a repealer of its own background-check regime.

current_implementation.detail: No Code text is being or should be carried forward for this repealed enactment.

source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/reports/laws/PL-017-137.md classifies the act as NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED.
codification/mass_migration/latest/sources/text/PL-017-137.txt repeals PL 17-137 in its entirety.
No PL-17-137 note or Code text appears in current XML.
recommended_actions: Retain only as historical repeal information.
Do not add a Code section or statutory note.
Do not infer operative text.

laws.title: Public Law 17-138 | Hatch Act Amendment Act (HA)
laws.final_statutory_text: Section 5(1)-(2) repeals and replaces 5 U.S.C. 7323 with a new subsection structure covering political activity restrictions and exceptions.
laws.provision_level_disposition: Direct amendment to 5 U.S.C. 7323; the section text itself is the operative object.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is intact and operative, and the current branch already reflects the revised 7323 text.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Section 5 is a direct replace-section-text operation against 5 U.S.C. 7323; no transfer or redesignation chain is involved.
laws.status_basis: The source memo says section 5(1)-(2) replaces 5 U.S.C. 7323, and the current XML already contains the revised subsections and the Pub. L. 17-138 amendment note.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML already contains the revised 5 U.S.C. 7323 text and the USAR note for Pub. L. 17-138; origin/main does not.

source_evidence: codification/decisions/PL-017-138.md says section 5(1)-(2) repeals and replaces 5 U.S.C. 7323.
usc/usc05.xml current branch shows the revised subsection text for 7323 and a Pub. L. 17-138 note.
git diff origin/main shows the 7323 language was changed on this branch.
recommended_actions: Retain the current 5 U.S.C. 7323 text and history note.
No further Code change is required for this law.
Remove any remaining Trello linkage if present outside XML.

laws.title: Public Law 18-139 | The Bye Bye Background Checks Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals Public Law 17-137 in its entirety and halts the background-check regime established by that earlier act.
laws.provision_level_disposition: Standalone repealer; preserved, if at all, as a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 6132.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is intact and permanent, and no later enactment in the reviewed set repeals it.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The enactment is a direct repeal of PL 17-137; no transfer or redesignation survives.
laws.status_basis: The law text is readable and permanent, but it does not provide a uniquely executable Code amendment. The source record for the law is current, and the better treatment is a statutory note.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML has the underlying section 6132 text, but no PL-18-139 statutory note or repeal-history entry.

source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/reports/laws/PL-018-139.md classifies the act as STATUTORY_NOTE at 2 U.S.C. 6132.
codification/laws/laws/PL-018-139/law.txt says Public Law 17-137 shall be repealed in its entirety.
usc/usc02.xml currently has 2 U.S.C. 6132 but no PL-18-139 note.
recommended_actions: Add a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 6132 recording the repeal of PL 17-137.
Do not add substantive Code text.
Do not treat the note as a new operative section.

laws.title: Public Law 19-144 | Public Area Management Act (PAMA)
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates District of Columbia road and sidewalk closure authority, defines district/public-road/private-road terms, sets closure reasons and limits, and repeals PL 15-95 section VI(2)-(4).
laws.provision_level_disposition: Standalone generally permanent law; best preserved as a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 5346.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is intact and permanent, and no later enactment in the reviewed set repeals it.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: The act contains a direct repeal of PL 15-95 section VI(2)-(4); no transfer or redesignation chain survives.
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and permanent, but it does not supply a uniquely executable Code amendment. The best treatment is a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 5346.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML has the preexisting section 5346 text, but no PL-19-144 statutory note has been added.

source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/reports/laws/PL-019-144.md classifies the act as STATUTORY_NOTE at 2 U.S.C. 5346.
codification/laws/laws/PL-019-144/law.txt contains the District closure authority and repeal of PL 15-95 section VI(2)-(4).
usc/usc02.xml currently has 2 U.S.C. 5346 but no PL-19-144 note.
recommended_actions: Add a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 5346.
Do not add substantive Code text.
Do not treat the note as a new operative section.
